title: Question about Monday Automations
excerpt: I am a bit confused by the Monday.com Automations.  Are these integrations the same integrations as the ones I am using in Zapier or are these proprietary integrations built by Monday.com?  I see different triggers and a…

posts.author: mavenllc (mavenllc)
posts.cookedHtml: I am a bit confused by the Monday.com Automations. Are these integrations the same integrations as the ones I am using in Zapier or are these proprietary integrations built by Monday.com? I see different triggers and actions across the two platforms. Also, if the Monday.com automation are proprietary, is there a roadmap of integrations that are coming?

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @mavenllc

Welcome to the community. I might be a little confusing. Automations are builtin automations (only monday development can build these). Integrations are either between external apps and monday or monday to monday. One of the integrations is “when xxx send a webhook”, where these webhooks can be services from a number of endpoints (Zapier, Integromat, or any other service on the web). The app developers can build other integrations. When the 3rd party developers build integraion apps (monday to monday) they look very much like the automations, although technically they are integrations

posts.author: dsilva (Daniel Silva)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @mavenllc - see comment above by Bas. Essentially integrations are built by monday development, whereas tools such as Zapier exists as a way to integrate other applications using something like Zapier / Integromat as the middleman.

-Daniel

posts.author: system (system)
posts.cookedHtml: This topic was automatically closed 7 days after the last reply. New replies are no longer allowed.
